zotero.abstractNote: The ability to interact with the environment is a fundamental requirement for autonomous agents. This requires the agent to understand which actions are possible in a given situation, a concept known as affordances. In this paper, we propose a framework for the inference of affordances and active motor control in simulated agents. We use a deep reinforcement learning approach where the agent learns to predict the outcome of its actions and to select actions that lead to desired outcomes. We demonstrate our approach in a simulated environment where an agent has to learn to interact with objects to achieve a goal. Our results show that the agent can learn to infer affordances and use them for active motor control, leading to more efficient and robust behavior.
